name: Naturepedia Soil Carbon Systems Map
description: Machine-readable relationship map for the Naturepedia Soil Carbon Systems™ family.
architecture: Soil Carbon Systems → Soil Organic Matter → Root Carbon Pathways → Mycorrhizal Carbon → Microbial Carbon Pump → Soil Aggregates → Humus Formation → Carbon Stabilization → Soil Respiration → Carbon Sequestration in Soil → Regenerative Soil Carbon

systemNodes.name: Soil Carbon Systems™
systemNodes.role: Parent system integrating biological, chemical, and physical carbon storage processes within living soils.

systemNodes.name: Soil Organic Matter
systemNodes.role: Organic residues, living biomass, decomposed matter, and long-term soil carbon pool.

systemNodes.name: Root Carbon Pathways
systemNodes.role: Carbon movement through roots, exudates, rhizosphere interactions, and belowground allocation.

systemNodes.name: Mycorrhizal Carbon
systemNodes.role: Fungal-mediated carbon transport, storage, and exchange within mycorrhizal networks.

systemNodes.name: Microbial Carbon Pump
systemNodes.role: Transformation of plant carbon into stable microbial biomass and mineral-associated organic matter.

systemNodes.name: Soil Aggregates
systemNodes.role: Physical protection of organic matter through aggregate formation and soil structure.

systemNodes.name: Humus Formation
systemNodes.role: Development of stable humified organic matter and long-term soil fertility.

systemNodes.name: Carbon Stabilization
systemNodes.role: Biological, mineral, fungal, and aggregate processes that retain carbon in soils.

systemNodes.name: Soil Respiration
systemNodes.role: Carbon dioxide release through root metabolism, microbial respiration, and decomposition.

systemNodes.name: Carbon Sequestration in Soil
systemNodes.role: Long-term storage of atmospheric carbon within living soil systems.

systemNodes.name: Regenerative Soil Carbon
systemNodes.role: Carbon rebuilding through regenerative agriculture, biodiversity, cover crops, compost, reduced disturbance, and healthy soil food webs.

relatedSystems.relationship: Provides the global carbon flow that feeds soil carbon storage and cycling.
Provides the physical and biological foundation for soil carbon dynamics.
Controls root allocation, carbon exudation, and belowground communication.
Distributes plant-derived carbon through fungal networks.
Transforms carbon into microbial biomass and stable soil organic matter.
Regulates soil moisture, erosion, infiltration, and protection of stored carbon.
Links soil carbon with resilience, biodiversity, nutrient cycling, and ecosystem stability.
Connects soil carbon storage and release to atmospheric carbon and climate regulation.
Extends soil carbon storage into forest ecosystems and woody biomass.
Integrates microbial metabolism with long-term carbon stabilization.
